content.problemStatement:
Chef has two numbers NNN and MMM. Help Chef to find number of integer NNN-tuples (A1,A2,…,AN)(A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N)(A1​,A2​,…,AN​) such that 0≤A1,A2,…,AN≤2M−10 \leq A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N \leq 2^M - 10≤A1​,A2​,…,AN​≤2M−1 and A1&A2&…&AN=0A_1 \& A_2 \& \ldots \& A_N = 0A1​&A2​&…&AN​=0, where &\&& denotes the bitwise AND operator.

Since the number of tuples can be large, output it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two integers N and M separated by a space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of N‑tuples (A1,…,AN) such that 0 ≤ Ai ≤ 2^M‑1 and (A1 & A2 & … & AN) = 0, modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case the answer is (2^N - 1)^M modulo 1e9+7. The computed values are: (2^1-1)^2 = 1, (2^2-1)^2 = 9, (2^4-1)^2 = 225, (2^8-1)^4 = 228250597.

solution.approach:
For each bit position (0 … M‑1) the N numbers can have any combination of 0/1 except the case where all N numbers have a 1 at that bit, because that would make the overall AND have a 1 at that position. Hence for a single bit there are 2^N – 1 valid assignments. Bits are independent, therefore the total number of tuples is (2^N – 1)^M. The answer is computed modulo MOD = 1 000 000 007 using fast exponentiation:
1. Compute p = 2^N mod MOD.
2. a = (p – 1 + MOD) % MOD.
3. answer = a^M mod MOD.
Both exponentiations are performed with binary exponentiation in O(log N + log M) time per test case.
